entry.title: Follow-Up Material Not Considered In MRP Run
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are discontinuing X packaging material from 01.07.2025 and we have maintained Y material as follow-up material in material master data. Currently we have stock of 8000 units for X material which covers the requirement till the date of 18.07.2025, so whatever requirements after this date should go on follow up material Y, but system is not creating any requirements for Y material when we run the MRP. We have created variant to run MRP which is based on the MRP controller, so the MRP controller for the Y material is same as we given in variant, still it's not creating any requirement. but when we run MRP only for Y material it's creating the requirements in system. I'm not understanding why it's happening like this. Can you pls provide your expertise here. Master data is also same for X & Y. Only issue is that when we run MRP for all packaging material that time it's not creating any requirement on follow up material 'Y' and when run MRP individually only for 'Y' material then it's creating the requirements.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Create Index on Global Temporary Table in S...

entry.title: Re: Cannot change Document Owner in SAP Business One vers...
entry.content:
Hi kedalenechong 

Could you please check if customer is using Data Ownership ?
if yes then According to the definition the only one who can change owner in the owner field in the header is a manager to himself or to his subordinates.

If an employee has no subordinates he will not be able to change it.

Kind Regards
Nidhi 
SAP Support

entry.title: About depreciation Area of 20
entry.content:
Dear all ：
In our system's Chinese chart of depreciation , a depreciation Area of 20 "cost accounting depreciation" has been set for  assets, and it have also been set for posting depreciation. However, an error was reported during the depreciation process. error is shown: "Account 'X-Y' not allowed to be defined as reconciliation account" - Message no. AC309. I followed the advice provided in SAP Note 1888597, -change message: AC309 from "Error" to "Warning" using transaction OBA5. Depreciation was carried out again and it was found that depreciation area 01 can be posted, but depreciation area 20 still reported the same error.
Does anyone know how to use the depreciation area of 20"cost accounting depreciation"? Does it need to be actually posted?
Why did I change this error message to a warning in OBA5, but it still reports a red light?
Thank you and best regards!
Wang

entry.title: Root Certificate Authority (CA) Update (*businessbydesign.cloud.sap) & (*sapbydesign.com)
entry.content:
Background

As part of the evolving landscape of internet security, compliance requirements, and our ongoing commitment to delivering secure and trusted services, Cloud Integration (ISUITE) will be updating the Root Certificate Authority (CA) used for load balancer certificates in both design-time and runtime environments.


What Is Changing?

To maintain compliance with the latest cryptographic standards, we are transitioning from the DigiCert Global Root G2 certificate to the DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5 certificate.

The DigiCert G5 Root represents the next generation of certificate infrastructure, leveraging a stronger SHA-256 signature algorithm (as opposed to the older SHA-1), providing enhanced protection and aligning with current industry best practices.

 

What Do I Need to Do?

If your systems:

  • Implement certificate pinning, or
  • Explicitly trust only certain root certificates (e.g., DigiCert Global Root G2)

You must ensure that the new DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5 certificate is added to your trusted certificate store before the migration begins to avoid any disruption in service communication.

 

Certificate Comparison

Below is a summary of the changes:

Certificate AuthorityNew (G5) CertificatesCurrent (G2) Certificates
DigiCertDigiCert G5 TLS RSA4096 SHA384 2021 CA1 (Intermediate)

DigiCert Global G2 TLS RSA SHA256 2020 CA1 (Intermediate)

 DigiCert TLS RSA4096 Root G5 (Root)DigiCert Global Root G2 (Root)


Download new certificate

Below are the steps to download new certificate:

    • Kindly click on download link. You will be redirected to Digi Cert Website, here ensure Combined Certificate Files is set as shown below.

 

    • Click download as shown below.

    • A zip file by name: star_businessbydesign_cloud_sap_1299509218 would be downloaded.
    • Please unzip this file and we can see required certificate: star_businessbydesign_cloud_sap.crt as shown below.

 

FAQs

1) What are these certificates used for?
These certificates are used for the SSL/TLS handshake that any system using the ‘secure’ protocol does before allowing connection to/from the system. In our case, SAP Business ByDesign uses the ‘secure’ HTTPS protocol and hence the SSL handshake is must for any system to connect to these URLs.

2) Are the new certificates known to modern web browsers?
DigiCert Root Certificates are automatically recognized by all common web browsers, mobile devices, and mail clients, therefore for browser scenarios there is nothing to do. The same is true if one relies on the standard sapjvm trust list.
The CA root certificate is included in:

    • SAP JVM patch level 8.1.035 or 7.1.054
    • Cloud Foundry buildpack SAP-Java (sap_java_buildpack) version 1.6.15

3) How do I download or install the certificate?
You must have admin access to the server where you need to install the certificate. If you do not have access to your company’s SSL server, notify your IT team and provide them the respective certificate download link from the above table.

4) How do Import Single Certificate in SAP CPI Key Store?
Follow the steps mentioned in the link.

5) How to check the certificate in my browser trust list?
Navigate to chrome://settings and scroll down to ‘Advanced’.

    • Under “Privacy and Security,” click “Manage Certificates.”
    • On the popup that was launched, select “Trusted Root Certification Authorities’. The certificate will be displayed there.


6) How to import the certificate into my browser?
Procedure

    • Open chrome browser.
    • Click Customize and control Google Chrome button in the upper right corner.
    • Choose Settings. …
    • Under Privacy and security section, click More. …
    • Click Manage certificates, The new window will appear. …
    • Choose Trusted Root Certification Authorities tab.
    • Click Import. …
    • In the opened window, click Next.


7) I notice a discrepancy in the validity start date and end date mentioned in this knowledge article table and my downloaded certificate. What does this indicate?
Sometimes, due to time zone difference, you may see a different date in the downloaded certificate. There is no impact on the certificate update activity due to this. You will be renewing the certificate well in advance, before the certificate expiry date.

 

Please do share feedback and your thoughts in the comment section below.
You can also refer SAP Business ByDesign environment.
Topic page: (https://community.sap.com/topics/business-bydesign)
Post and answer questions (https://answers.sap.com/tags/01200615320800000691)
Read other posts on the topic (https://blogs.sap.com/tags/01200615320800000691/)

entry.title: Get Started Quickly with SAP Central Business Configuration in One Article
entry.content:
SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC) enables you to scope, configure and implement end-to-end business processes for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition from one main place. Let's now delve into the world of CBC!

Depending on your installation, your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is based either on a 2-system landscape or on a 3-system landscape. In future, customer systems with SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2-system landscape and without SAP Central Business Configuration (use of Manage Your Solution and Configure Your Solution) are going to be converted to SAP Central Business Configuration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-system landscape. Besides, customers who have an SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2-system landscape with SAP Central Business Configuration are going to be converted to an SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-system landscape as well. This article only focuses on CBC for 3-system landscape.

Related Information:

  • How will relevant customer systems be converted to SAP Central Business Configuration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-system landscape?
  • System Landscapes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

In CBC, there are six main activities that you need to complete for 3-system landscape.

1. Assign Deployment Target

A deployment target represents a dedicated system tenant of the assigned solution. To exchange content from your workspaces to the leading product, you must specify a deployment target in the Deployment Target app, where deployment targets are listed and can be assigned or removed.

When opening the app, you see a list with deployment targets that are available and provisioned by SAP cloud operations. You can only assign deployment targets that are not assigned to any other workspace. The available deployment targets also depend whether you work with an evaluation or implementation workspace.

One of the following systems can be connected to evaluation workspace:

  • Starter system
  • Partner demo system (separate license required)
  • Customer sandbox system (separate license required)
  • Training system

For implementation workspaces, one workspace will be connected to the customizing tenant (client 100), and the other workspace will be connected to the development tenant (client 80) of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud development system.

In the Assign Deployment Target dialog, you can select Include Demo Data (available for certain systems): You can select whether to include demo data or not. Be aware that this is a one-time decision. You cannot add any demo data at a later stage.

Related Information:

  • KBA 3571722 - Cannot find deployment target under Implementation Workspace in SAP Central Business Configuration
  • Request the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Development System
  • SAP Help Portal: Creating a New Workspace
  • SAP Help Portal: Systems and Demo Data

2. Define Scope

In Define Scope app, you select the countries/regions you operate in, then you determine which of the available bundles and scenarios, languages you want to incorporate into your solution.

Pay attention to the filter. If it is set incorrectly, it may cause you to fail to search for the scope item you want to add. If you don't search for a certain scope item, please try to clear all the filters then search again.

License-dependent scenarios and some non-standard scenarios are marked with lock icon. To request these scenarios, you need to create a support case in component XX-S4C-OPR-SRV.  KBA 3122430 - Scope item activation request for SAP Central Business Configuration       

Note:

  1. Some scope items can only be added in initial scoping phase. Once completing and confirming scoping by choosing Complete Activity button in Define Scope app, they cannot be added anymore. KBA 3425018 - How to check whether a scope item is only available for initial scoping
  2. After confirming your scope, you cannot delete any countries/regions, bundles, or scenarios. These choices are irreversible.

3. Specifying Primary Finance Settings

In this app you need to specify the fiscal year variant and group currency. If you have included demo data in your system, you can only choose K4 - Cal. Year, 4 Special Periods as fiscal year variant and USD as group currency. Specifying the primary finance settings is a one-time decision. The settings cannot be changed after being saved.

Related Information:

  • SAP Help Portal: Systems and Demo Data
  • SAP Help Portal: Specifying Primary Finance Settings

4. Setting Up Organizational Structure

The Set Up Organizational Structure app provides an intuitive way which allows a graphical, template based, scope dependent creation of organizational units.

Units to Create and Units to Confirm indicators: these two indicators show the number of organizational units you need to create and confirm. You can choose them to see the detailed list of organizational units to be created or confirmed. These mandatory organizational entities are in accordance to the selected Scenarios during Scoping.

When there are mandatory organizational units to be created, the Units to Create indicator is red, the Units to Confirm indicator is grayed out, as well as the Confirm button and the Complete Activity button. After all mandatory organizational units are created, the Units to Create indicator turns green and the Units to Confirm indicator turns red, with the number of organizational units needed to be confirmed, and you can proceed to confirm your organizational structure with the Confirm button. KBA 3165028 - Mandatory organizational units to be created and confirm button in CBC activity "Set up Organizational Structure" is grayed out

Note:

  • Once an organizational unit or assignment is confirmed, it cannot be deleted or unassigned.
  • KBA 3537843- Frequently asked questions in the organization management area n SAP Central Business Configuration. It might help you find answers to some of the common questions that are already answered.

5. Deploying Scope and Organizational Structure

Adding scope items and creating organizational structure in CBC does not mean the content will be activated into your target S/4HANA Cloud system. To deploy the scope and organizational structure to your target system, go to the Deploy Scope and Organizational Structure activity and choose Deploy at the end of the row.

A progress bar displays the progression of the process. The percentage reaches 100% only once both steps of the process are completed.

  1. Deployment from SAP Central Business Configuration to the deployment target.
  2. Scope activation in the deployment target.

Please note that the process may take up to several hours. KBA 3288076 - Milestone Confirm Scope and Organizational Structure Phase is Completed stuck at 60% progress

Note:

  • You can delete a workspace before completing the Deploying Scope and Organizational Structure activity. After the content is deployed to the target system, the workspace can not be deleted. More information, see Deleting a Workspace.
  • Once your scope is deployed from SAP Central Business Configuration and activated in your customizing tenant (100) of development system, the transport of type Central Business Configuration in the app Export Customizing Transports is available. You release these transport requests from the customizing tenant (100) by using the app Export Customizing Transports. You import all types of transport request into the test tenant (100) of your test system, and forward/import them into the production tenant (100) of your production system, by using the app Import Collection. More information, see SAP Help Portal: Transports

6. Configure Your Business Processes

To adjust system settings to your business requirements you carry out configuration activities in the Configure Your Business Processes app. The available configuration activities are based on the active scope (business processes) and country/region selections. Configuration activities may vary by country, because not all business processes are available in all countries.

Note : To process a configuration activity, you must have corresponding authorizations in the target system as well. You can follow KBA 3238660 to find the business catalog to access a SSCUI (Configuration Activity) and avoid authorization errors.     

Select the link in the Title column to open the configuration activity, enter the values necessary, and select Save. If you've added new data or removed/edited existing data, you'll be prompted to assign the change to a transport after you click the Save button. This ensures all values added, removed, or changed are captured in a transport. When prompted to assign configuration activity changes to a customizing request, either search for an existing request you've already created with the Own Requests button, or select the "document" icon to create a new request. Enter the details of the new request, and save the change to the customizing request.

You release these transport requests from the customizing tenant (100) by using the app Export Customizing Transports. Import them into the test tenant (100) of your test system, and forward/import them into the production tenant (100) of your production system, by using the app Import Collection. More information, see SAP Help Portal: Transports

Some configuration activities in SAP Central Business Configuration are not able to be transported to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud test or production systems. They must be changed regularly and only valid for the system in which you make them. We call these "current settings". Since SAP Central Business Configuration is only connected to the development system tenants, not the test or production system, we have to use the app Implementation Activities to make current settings directly in your other systems. You can also use this app to compare content either from the test and production system with the development system, or you can choose to compare either the test or production system with the development system. More information, see Implementation Activities.

 

It is hoped that this article can help you quickly understand the main functions of CBC.

entry.title: Query modelling in SAP DATASPHERE with use case example
entry.content:
Manipulating queries in SAP Datasphere (formerly SAP Data Warehouse Cloud) involves working within its SQL-based modeling environment, graphical views, and business layer. Depending on your objective—transforming data, filtering, joining, or creating calculated columns—here are the primary ways to manipulate queries in Datasphere:

Here is a solution to how we can manipulate data with simple SQL queries that can be used to manipulate data results:

- Graphical View Modeling (No-Code/Low-Code)

SAP Datasphere offers a graphical modeling environment to build views (analytical, calculation, or relational). You can manipulate queries visually by:

  • Adding Filters: Apply filters on specific fields (e.g., Region = ‘US’) in the semantics or projection nodes.
  • Creating Calculated Columns: Add new derived columns using formulas (like IF, CASE, arithmetic).
  • Joining Tables: Drag and connect tables using inner, left outer, or full joins.
  • Aggregating Data: Apply aggregation (e.g., SUM(Sales)) at group or table level.
  • Input Parameters & Variables: Add prompts for runtime filtering.
- SQL View Modeling (Code-Based) (Simple)

Datasphere supports SQL-based views using standard SQL and SQL extensions. Here's how you can manipulate queries programmatically:

a. Create Filters

SELECT * FROM SalesData

WHERE Region = 'APAC' AND Year = 2024;

b. Create Calculated Fields

SELECT OrderID, Quantity, UnitPrice, Quantity * UnitPrice AS Revenue

FROM Orders;

c. Joins

SELECT a.CustomerID, a.Name, b.SalesAmount

FROM Customers a

JOIN Sales b ON a.CustomerID = b.CustomerID;

d. Using Parameters

Define reusable SQL views that take input parameters:

SELECT * FROM Orders

WHERE OrderDate >= :StartDate AND OrderDate <= :EndDate;

You define parameters in the View Properties > Parameters section.

 

- Use Business Builder for Semantics (Deep Dive provided in the next section)

The Business Builder allows data modeling with business semantics:

Rename technical fields (e.g., MATNR → Product ID)

Define hierarchies and associations

Build Consumption Models that serve as the final layer for analytics tools (like SAP Analytics Cloud)

 

- Manipulate Query Runtime Behavior

Use Variables: Define filters users can change at runtime (e.g., time ranges, cost centers)

Apply Restricted Measures: For example, filter a measure for a specific product group

Create Aggregation Levels: Define the level at which KPIs should roll up (e.g., by quarter, not month)

Example: Combine and Filter Customer & Sales Data

SELECT c.CustomerName, s.SalesAmount, s.Region

FROM Customers c

JOIN Sales s ON c.CustomerID = s.CustomerID

WHERE s.Region = 'Europe' AND s.Year = 2025;

You can turn this into a SQL View, then expose it via a Consumption Model with input parameters like Region and Year.

Step-by-Step Guide for a customer table example:

We assume two tables:

  • CUSTOMERS
CustomerID, CustomerName, Region
  • SALES
SalesID, CustomerID, Product, Quantity, UnitPrice, SalesDate

Step 1: Create a SQL View in Datasphere

  1. Go to Data Builder.
  2. Click Create → SQL View.
  3. Name it SALES_ANALYSIS_VIEW.

Paste the SQL code:

Click Save and Deploy the view.

Step 2: Add Input Parameters (Optional)

  1. In the SQL View editor, click Parameters tab.
  2. Add parameter P_REGION (String).
  3. Modify SQL:
...

WHERE

    c.Region = :P_REGION

Now it's dynamic user can choose the region at runtime.

Step 3: Create a Business Layer Consumption Model

  1. Go to Business Builder.
  2. Click Create Consumption Model.
  3. Add the SQL View SALES_ANALYSIS_VIEW as the source.
  4. Configure:
    • Dimensions: CustomerName, Region, Product
    • Measures: Revenue
    • Add P_REGION as an input prompt
  5. Save and publish the model.

Step 4: Consume in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)

  1. Open SAC → Create Story → Use SAP Datasphere live connection.
  2. Choose your SALES_ANALYSIS_VIEW Consumption Model.
  3. SAC prompts for P_REGION → select value like “North America”.
  4. Visualize:
    • Bar chart: Revenue by Product
    • Table: Revenue by Customer

How to Use the Business Builder in detail:

Open Business Builder (Logged into Datasphere- Needs license)

  • Go to Business Catalog → Select Business Builder
  • Click Create → Business Entity

Create Business Entities

  • Choose base view (Graphical or SQL View)
  • Map technical fields to business names
  • Define primary keys, descriptions, and labels

Create Associations

  • Example: In Sales Entity, associate CustomerID to the Customer Entity
  • You can now drill from Sales to Customer details in reports

Define Measures & Attributes

  • Add calculated fields (e.g., Revenue = Quantity * UnitPrice)
  • Define aggregation behavior (sum, count, etc.)

Build a Consumption Model

  • Combine entities like Customers, Sales, Products
  • Select fields for display
  • Add filters, input parameters (e.g., Region, Date Range)
  • Define KPIs like Total Sales, Average Order Value

Publish to Analytics

  • Once saved and deployed, Consumption Models appear in SAP Analytics Cloud
  • SAC users get clean, business-ready models with consistent logic

Business Builder Benefits:

Benefit

Explanation

Business Semantic Layer

Translates technical models into business terms

No SQL Needed

Drag-and-drop interface to build models

Reduced Complexity

Hide joins, filters, and calculated fields from end users

Security Enforcement

Add row-level or column-level access rules

Reusability

Share standard metrics (like KPIs) across models

Analytics Ready

Creates models ready for SAC, Excel, or external APIs

Final Thoughts:

Queries should be written in a good way such that the performance is not hampered. Every example is different and a common protocol cannot be followed for every single use case. 

Please work with subject matter experts to form high performance queries.

entry.title: Re: Unable to create notification in SAP Asset Manager
entry.content:
I managed to fix this for my SSAM2405. We use our own Notification Priorities and due to wrong SSAM Config the oMDO SAM2405_NOTIF_TYPE -> NOTIF_PRIORITY_TYPE. Because our Z-Priorities were not configured properly the oData-Service could not provide any Priority and within the NotificatioNCreateUpdate.page the Library beneath tries to read the value of the Priority field which is empty because the oMDO didnt deliver any Priorities. That then triggers the generic error because it's generic in the way that the Library tries to fetch an item for a non-existing field. 

That was the magic in my case. Good luck everyone and hope this helps.

  • In Public Cloud, certain master data like Plant Address is maintained via the Business Partner app, not SSCUIs.

SAP KBA Reference

SAP Note 3086829 – Restrictions in Public Cloud Configuration - SAP Note 3086829 (SAP login required)

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud SM: Mass Upload of Process Instances
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community members! 

I'm excited to share the improvements made to the Process Management screen in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model (SAP PaPM Cloud SM). To improve efficiency when working with complex processes that contain a large number of similar activities, a new functionality has been introduced: Mass Upload of Process Settings. This feature enables users to download the configuration of existing process instances in the form of an XLSX file, make changes in required sheets, and then upload those changes back into the application to create multiple new instances simultaneously. 

Two new buttons have been added to the top toolbar – Download Process Settings and Upload Process Settings. The first enables users to download the configuration of existing instances as a template for further use, while the second is used for uploading a prepared XLSX file with new process instances. 

Downloading Process Instances 

To download the configuration of an existing process instance, select one or more instances from the list and click the Download Process Settings button. If only one instance is selected, a single XLSX file containing its configuration will be downloaded. In case multiple instances are selected, a ZIP file containing one XLSX file per instance will be downloaded.  

The name of the downloaded file will include the environment ID, version, and process instance ID, for example, RPM_TS05_PROC07.  

Each XLSX file has the same structure and includes five sheets:  

  • Process - Contains general information about process instances, including the following columns: Process, Description, Process Type, Process State, and Start Date. 
  • Activities - Contains information about activities from selected process instances, including the following columns: Process, Activity, Description, Performer, Reviewer, Start Date, and End Date.
  • Parameters - Contains information about defined parameters with the following columns: Process, Field, and Value.
  • Selections - Contains information about the defined selection with the following columns: Process, Selection Field, Logical Operator, Operator Type, Selection Min. Value and Selection Max. Values. For selections that don't require a minimum and maximum value, the value under Selection Min. Value will be taken as the selection value during upload.
  • Help - Provides additional information that might be useful when configuring the other four tabs. This tab is not considered during the upload process. 

While the structure and column order are always the same, the row content varies depending on which instance is selected – i.e., which activities, parameters, and selections it contains. 

Configuration of the XLSX file: 

  1. The XLSX file must maintain its structure exactly without changing the order of the columns, otherwise, the content cannot be placed correctly. 
  2. The Process State field on the Process sheet and the Logical Operator and Operator Type fields on the Selections sheet have dropdown menus, allowing you to easily select predefined available options. You can find more information about these values on the Help sheet. 
  3. The uploaded file should only contain activity, parameters and selections that exist and are active in the target process instance. If an XLSX file contains some elements that are not defined in the target process template, the upload process will be canceled. 
  4. If any elements (parameters, process selections, activities) that are part of the process template are not mentioned in the XLSX file, they will be copied from the target process instance. 
  5. If a field is defined in the Parameter or Selection sheet but has no value, it will be configured as an empty field. 
  6. Data is linked to the Process ID in the first column of each sheet. Rows with the same Process ID will be used to create a single process instance, and subsequent rows will be used accordingly. 
  7. The maximum number of process instances that can be uploaded within a single XLSX file is 50. If you exceed this limit, the system will automatically cancel the upload and display an appropriate error message. 

Uploading Process Instances 

Once you have finished configuring new instances in the XLSX file, the next step is to upload the file back into the PaPM application. On the Process Management screen, you first need to select one base instance, either the one for which you downloaded process settings or another instance with the same configuration, meaning using the same process template, so they contain the same Activity, Parameters and Selection fields. Clicking the Upload Process Settings button opens a file upload dialog, where you select the XLSX file that was configured in the previous steps. After confirming, the application will create new instances based on the data from all four tabs. 

It is important to note that the upload will not be successful if there is any error in the data, even if just one instance in the file is invalid, the entire upload process will be canceled, and no instances will be created. In such cases, a red toast message will appear, notifying you of the failure. For more details about the error, refer to the Message Log. 

Note: For the value field, no data validation will occur during the upload process, meaning the value field will not be checked if the values satisfy the defined configuration for that field. The process instance will be uploaded successfully without displaying any warning messages. This type of error can only be seen after uploading and expanding specific process instances, parameters, or selections, depending on where the error is present.   

The new Mass Upload of Process Settings functionality significantly speeds up the configuration of multiple similar processes, reduces manual work, and enables better standardization in the SAP PaPM Cloud SM environment. 

I hope you find these updates helpful!  

Stay tuned for the latest updates and insights in the next one. Thank you!

entry.title: Migration story of 48 SAP SuccessFactors HCM successful integrations in 4 Months
entry.content:
1. Purpose of the document:

This document details how we migrated 48 SAP SuccessFactors HCM Boomi integrations to SAP Integration Suite successfully in 4 Months. We explain the strategy, execution, and different types of integration migration. 

2. Why SAP SuccessFactors HCM customer needs to migrate from Boomi to SAP Integration Suite: 

  1. Between 2013 and 2016, SAP SuccessFactors HCM customers used BOOMI as an integration platform to support data integration requirements in SAP SuccessFactors HCM implementation, and many customers are still running on Boomi
  2. SAP Customers have significantly invested in SAP BTP and SAP Integration Suite in the last seven years as part of S4HANA transitions and Integration Modernization (moving from SAP PO to SAP IS). 
  3. Customers are migrating interfaces from Boomi to the SAP Integration Suite, aligning with the SAP Integration strategy.
  4. Maintaining multiple integration platforms adds a lot of Technical Debt, and SAP customers consolidate and move to one integration platform (SAP Integration Suite) 

3. SAP SuccessFactors- Boomi Integration Landscape:

The customer implemented SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central in 2015 and utilized the BOOMI Integration Platform, building 48 integrations to support all Integration requirements.

 

 

4. Boomi to SAP Integration Suite Migration –  Framework:

Created a detailed migration framework to cover end to end execution 

 

 

Interface Mapping : Existing BOOMI interfaces evaluated  and mapped

 

 

5. Interface Development Approach Decision Process and Guidelines: 

 

 

  1. We implement prepackaged integrations developed by SAP to reduce development effort and customization. 
  2. We use SAP SuccessFactors Integration Center (IC) Templates for all simple to moderately complex Integrations.  
  3. We have built reusable assets to reuse . 
  4. Have used SF OData APIs. 
  5. Have checked with vendors about API integration capabilities to avoid the SFTP files approach. 
  6. Followed Real Time - Intelligent Services Based Event Driven approach wherever possible. 
  7. Strong governance in place and followed clean core capabilities and leveraged SAP IS standard capabilities. 
  8. Built reusable assets, and every consultant followed the same Integration standards. 
  9. Documented detailed technical specification document, created an integration design process and mapping document.

6. SFEC to SAP ERP Integrations Migration :

The customer was using custom Boomi integrations to replicate Emp and Org data, and manual file imports to update bank master data and cost center data.

  1. Implemented prepackaged integrations developed by SAP to integrate SAP SuccessFactors EC to SAP ERP – Employee and Org data
  2. Implemented Cost Center Prepackaged Integration to replicate Cost Center data from SAP ERP to SF EC
  3. Implemented Prepackaged Integration Template to replicate Bank Master Data from SAP ERP to SF EC

 

 

7. Instance Strategy and Interface Code Management :

 The customer had 3 BOOMI atoms, and we recommended that the customer get the 3 Integration Suite tenants and used the same.

  1. Have used SAP Cloud Transport Management to transport the code and implemented the governance process to make sure the the Integration Code will not transport without approval from the the business owner 

 

 

2. Cloud to On-Premise Connectivity

  • Customer did not use Boomi Local Atoms  - Hence, we didn’t go with Edge integration cell but used SAP Cloud Connector for SAP ERP integrations
  • Established connectivity with SFTP servers and used SSH connectivity option, and implemented Encryption
  • SAP IS IP ranges info shared with Vendors,, and they have whitelisted the same to have a a successful handshake

 

 

3. SAP SuccessFactors HCM - SAP Integration Suitelandscape after Migration

 

 

  1. Workforce Software Integrations migration – Implemented new prepackaged integration delivered by Workforce Software to replicate employee data from SF EC to WFS and used SAP Prepackaged integration to replicate Time and Absences data to EC Payroll
  2. ServiceNow Integrations – Implemented Prepackaged Integration templates to replicate Employee and Contingents data using SAP IS – APIs approach
  3. Tableau Integration – Tableau is directly integrated with SF EC and imported employee and Org data – Direct Integration
  4. OC Tanner Integration - Implemented Prepackaged Integration templates to replicate Employee Data to OC Tanner
  5. ComputerShare Integration - Implemented Prepackaged Integration templates to replicate Employee Data to Computer Share
  6. SailPoint Integrations – SailPoint built a direct integration to integrate SAP SuccessFactors EC – SailPoint, and used the same replicate Employee, Org, and Foundation data and matched the same file layout as Boomi
  7. Qualtrics Integration - Implemented Prepackaged Integration templates to replicate Employee Data to Qualtrics
  8. Data Hub – Explored option to integrate Data Hub with SF EC directly APIs, but significant design changes and development required at Data Hub, hence built five master integrations to replicate Employee, Org, and Foundation data and matched the same file layout as Boomi
  9. Internal Systems Integrations – Generate the same file layouts as BOOMI but used  integration templates 

 8.  Key Considerations: 

 

 

  1. Engaging an SAP Integration Suite expert who is familiar with the Boomi integration platform is key.
  2. Migrating interfaces in a phased manner is a good idea
  3. Use this migration as an opportunity to reduce the tech debt and implement prepackaged integrations or Direct Integrations
  4. Creating good documentation is key, and delivering the requirement, technical design, and data mapping document is mandatory for each integration
  5. Make sure every part of the project follows SAP Integration Development standards and SF APIs leading practices
  6. Plan enough time to perform testing
  7. Build an internal team that can validate SI’s work
  8. Reduce the impact on vendors and make sure Boomi and SAP IS generate the same files

entry.title: Gamechanger Alert: Meet SAP Joule Studio for Real-World Impact
entry.content:
As someone who's still fairly new to SAP and learning about all it has to offer, I recently came across SAP Joule Studio—a tool that honestly surprised me. It’s part of SAP’s growing focus on AI, but instead of being just a tech buzzword, it’s something practical. Something helpful. Something that actually makes life easier.

So I thought I’d share what I’ve learned so far, for anyone else who’s curious like me.

 

What is SAP Joule Studio?

SAP Joule Studio is a low-code/no-code platform that lets you create AI-powered agents—little digital helpers that live inside your SAP system and support everyday tasks. These agents can:

  • Answer questions using real SAP data
  • Send reminders or alerts
  • Trigger workflows based on events
  • Help users navigate complex processes

The best part? You don’t need to be a developer to build one.

All you need is a good understanding of a business problem, and Joule Studio helps you turn that into a solution using easy visual tools.

 

Why This Is a Game-Changer (With Real Use Cases)

What really got me excited was seeing how real-world teams can use this tool to solve day-to-day challenges. Here are a few examples that really stood out to me:

✅HR & Employee Self-Service

  • Use Case: An AI agent that answers common HR questions like “How many vacation days do I have left?”, “What’s the process to update my bank details?”, or “When does my probation end?”
  • Impact: Reduces emails to HR, gives instant answers, and improves employee satisfaction.

✅Onboarding Assistant

  • Use Case: An AI agent that guides a new joiner through their first week—showing them how to complete paperwork, introducing them to teammates, and sending daily checklists.
  • Impact: Makes onboarding smoother, especially for remote hires or global teams.

✅Procurement Alerts

  • Use Case: A smart assistant that watches for delays from key suppliers and notifies procurement managers if a PO is stuck or overdue.
  • Impact: Keeps procurement responsive and improves relationships with vendors.

✅Project Management Summary

  • Use Case: A daily AI briefing that tells project leads about overdue tasks, timesheet gaps, or resource bottlenecks.
  • Impact: Saves hours of manual tracking and keeps teams aligned.

✅Compliance Checker

  • Use Case: A bot that checks which employees haven't completed their mandatory training or signed policy documents, and sends them a reminder.
  • Impact: Helps HR stay compliant without manual follow-ups.

✅IT Helpdesk Agent

  • Use Case: An agent that answers questions like “How do I reset my SAP password?” or “Where do I raise a ticket?”
  • Impact: Cuts down ticket volume for IT support and empowers users.

These aren’t just theoretical—they’re real issues we see in companies every day. And Joule Studio gives you a way to solve them quickly, without coding or waiting on IT.

 

Why Clients Will Love It Too

For clients, Joule Studio brings:

  • Self-service power to their SAP users
  • Less dependency on IT for small tasks
  • Smarter workflows with reminders, alerts, and guidance
  • Faster onboarding and training for new team members
  • Consistency across geographies and business units

AI becomes something helpful, not something scary or complex. It empowers users instead of overwhelming them.

 

Where You Can See It in Action

If you're like me and prefer learning visually, I highly recommend this official demo:

 SAP Joule Studio – AI for Everyone (YouTube)

You can also explore SAP’s official product page for more examples and how-to guides:
 SAP Joule Studio – Official Page

These are great starting points with visuals and real examples.

 

Getting Started with Joule Studio

If you’re ready to try it:

  1. Make sure your system is running on SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform).
  2. Go into SAP Build and access Joule Studio.
  3. Start by creating a small AI agent for a common task.
  4. Train it using real process examples and test in Preview mode.
  5. Roll it out with the right user permissions.

 

 

 

Remember—you don’t need to be technical. You just need to understand your business and what people are struggling with. Joule Studio helps you close that gap.

 

Final Thoughts (From One Learner to Another)

I’m still exploring SAP, still learning, and still asking questions. But tools like Joule Studio give me hope, not just for where tech is going, but for how we can make it work for people.

This isn’t about replacing humans—it’s about supporting them with small, smart helpers that remove friction and free up time.

If you're also exploring SAP tools and wondering where to start with AI, this might just be the right place. Try building a simple use case and see what it unlocks for your team.

And if you’ve tried it, I’d really love to hear what you’ve built. Let’s share and learn together, because the best ideas often come from the people closest to the problem.

 

#SAPJoule, #JouleStudio, #SAPBTP, #AIinSAP, #Gamechanger, #NoCodeAI, #SAPBuild, #EnterpriseAI, #DigitalTransformation, #SAPCommunity

entry.title: Seamless Integration: Consuming SAP API Hub APIs in On-Premise SAP Applications
entry.content:
Have you ever got a client requirement to use SAP API hub API in SAP object and struggled to find a correct approach. It happened with me as well. Therefore, I decided to share step by step instructions to handle it with all of you.

 

Add Product Master API in SAP Gateway

 

  1. Register Product Master API in SAP GATEWAY

 

2. Enter the System Alias and External Service Name and click Get Services.

3. Select the service and Click Add Selected Services.

4. Enter package details and continue.

5. Filter gateway with added services.

6. Click SAP Gateway Client to test the service.

7. Click Execute.

Utilize this Odata service in Standard Badi

  1. Create implementation for Badi BADI_MATERIAL_CHECK

Go to SE19 >> Enter BADI name

Click Display

2. Go to Implementation >> Create

3. Provide implementation name and continue.

Add short text and Click Save

Add package details

4. Implement method

Go to Interface >> Check Data

 METHOD if_ex_badi_material_check~check_data.
**********************************************
*   Purpose: Call Product Master API         *
*--------------------------------------------*
*   Author : Sangeeta Singh                  *
**********************************************

    DATA: lv_matnr         TYPE matnr,
          lo_http_client   TYPE REF TO if_http_client,
          lv_request_url   TYPE string,
          lv_response_data TYPE string,
          lv_url           TYPE string.

    CONSTANTS: lc_url TYPE string VALUE 'https://<your_s4hana_host>:<port>/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_PRODUCT_SRV/'.
    " **CRITICAL: Replace with your actual service URL**

    "Get material number
    lv_matnr = wmara-matnr.
    lv_url = lc_url.

    "Run API call if material found in above step
    IF lv_matnr IS NOT INITIAL.
      CALL METHOD cl_http_client=>create_by_url
        EXPORTING
          url    = lv_url
        IMPORTING
          client = lo_http_client
        EXCEPTIONS
          OTHERS = 4.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        lo_http_client->request->set_method( 'GET' ).
        lo_http_client->send( ).
        lo_http_client->receive( ).
        lv_response_data = lo_http_client->response->get_cdata( ).
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

5. Save and Activate.

Test API

  1. Go to T-Code MM02

Enter any available material

2. Select Basic data

3. Change description and click Save.

Obviously, it is a very basic implementation, much more can be done. However, it is good to start with.

Thanks for vising!

Keep learning!!

Sangeeta Singh

Website

Linkedin
Medium
ABAP Development   SAP Community  API API Management

entry.title: How to Connect a Third-Party Application to SAP CPI Using APIs and Access Token – A GreytHR Example
entry.content:
👉   Introduction
In modern integration scenarios, many third-party applications expose APIs to exchange data. SAP
Integration Suite (CPI) allows us to consume these APIs easily, even when token-based authentication is
required.
In this blog, I’ll walk you through how I integrated the GreytHR HRMS application with SAP using API
endpoints and access tokens. We'll fetch employee master data using a secure, token-based flow.

👉Topics Covered
• What is an API First Approach?
• What are APIs?
• Types of APIs.
• using API How to connect third party Applications.

•  What is an API First Approach?
The API First Approach is an approach to software design that focuses on the API to create applications that can be easily connected to each other. API First creates ecosystems of applications that are modular, reusable, and extensible, just like Lego bricks.


An API First Approach means that your APIs are treated as first class citizens. Everything revolves around the end product being used by mobile devices and client applications. An API First approach involves developing APIs that are consistent and reusable. This is achieved by using an API description language.

                                       

The graphic shows an example of API First Approach. Information is provided in the following text.
Explanation:


API Provider :
An API provider provides an interface with technical features (No. 1). Symbolically, this is shown in this picture with the ball- or lollipop notation. This interface can be consumed by a service (No. 5). There presentation corresponds to the socket notation (No. 2). The service acts in the role of the API consumer.

API Consumer :
The API consumer requires an interface (No. 3). The representation corresponds to the socket notation (No. 4). In this case, the API provider is the service (No. 5). The presentation is again made with the ball-or lollipop notation.

Find More Information at:
Understanding the API-First Approach to Building Products.
It is not Cloud first or API first but Strategy first. API Management Strategy in Mult iCloud Environments | SAP Blogs.

• What are APIs?
API stands for Application Programming Interface. An API specifies the operations as well as inputs and outputs of a software component. It defines functionalities that are independent of their respective implementations, so that these implementations can vary without affecting the user of the API.


• Types of APIs.
In the literature, there are many overviews of different types of APIs. Below is a simple overview of the APIs that we must understand for this course.

The graphic shows an overview of APIs. Information is provided in the following text.
Here, four different APIs are defined under the superset of APIs, based on their use.
Databased APIs These are intended for file exchange between systems. Files can be, for example, configuration files.


Object-orientated APIs
These are used in object-oriented programming languages to define the communication of the classes with each other.
Remote APIs (No. 1)
This includes today's important web APIs, such as REST and SOAP APIs. REST itself is not a protocol but a software architectural style.
Messaging APIs (No. 2)
•  These are asynchronous APIs that send events based on events. These are used in Event Driven
architectures.


• using API How to connect Third party Applications.
I am trying to connect the GreytHR application to SAP CPI using APIs.
Step-1
✅ Step 1: Create API in GreytHR
• Log into your GreytHR portal
• Navigate to the API Management section
• Click Create API
• Provide:

• API Name
• Role access (select “All Roles”)

Click on Next Save the generated Username and Password

✅ Step 2: Store Credentials in CPI
• In SAP Integration Suite, go to:
• Monitoring → Manage Security → Security Material
• Create a User Credential
• Enter the username and password received from GREYTHR
• Name it: GREYTHR_CREDENTIALS

✅ Step 3: Create Access Token Flow in CPI
• Open your design workspace and create a new Integration Flow
• Use a Timer as the start event (for scheduled execution)
• Add a Request-Reply block
• Use an HTTP Adapter with:
• Method: POST
• URL: GreytHR’s Access Token Endpoint
• Authentication: use GREYTHR_CREDENTIALS

✅ Step 4: Convert JSON to XML (if necessary) and Store Access Token
If the response is in JSON format (which is common for most token APIs):
• Add a JSON to XML Converter immediately after the Request-Reply step
• This ensures the token response can be processed in SAP CPI (which primarily works with XML)
If the response is already in XML format, you can skip the converter step
• Add a Content Modifier after the token call
• Extract the ACCESS_TOKEN from the response JSON
• Store it as a property: ${header . ACCESS_TOKEN}

✅ Step 5: Call GreytHR API to Fetch Data
• Add another Request-Reply block
• HTTP Adapter configuration:
• Method: GET or POST
• URL: GREYTHR API endpoint for employee data
• Authorization: Bearer ${header. ACCESS_TOKEN}

Deploy the iFlow
• If successful, you’ll receive employee master data from GREYTHR!

 👉 Conclusion
• Using APIs and token-based authentication, SAP CPI can securely connect to third-party systems
like GreytHR. This approach is:
• Scalable
• Reusable
• Suitable for any modern integration scenario
The same logic can be extended to tools like Salesforce, Zoho, or OpenWeatherMap.

entry.title: Shaping the Future of Predictive Insights with Tabular AI and the SAP Foundational Model
entry.content:
Abstract

At SAP Sapphire, we unveiled a major milestone in our ongoing mission to simplify and elevate AI capabilities for our customers: Tabular AI. This groundbreaking initiative consolidates all of SAP’s existing AI services for structured tabular business data into a single, unified offering within SAP’s Generative AI Hub. Scheduled for general availability in the second half of 2025, tabular AI will streamline integration, enhance accessibility, and dramatically reduce complexity for developers and business users alike.

Beyond unification, tabular AI is designed as a launchpad for rapid innovation—setting the stage for next-generation capabilities like the SAP Foundation Model. Initially, these foundation model features will be available for embedded use cases, with a vision to expand to custom scenarios in the future, giving customers even greater flexibility.

If you’re new to the SAP Foundation Model we encourage you to explore our dedicated blog post and the on-demand SAP TechEd session for a comprehensive introduction. These resources provide valuable context on how foundational AI technologies are reshaping business applications.

Tabular AI underscores SAP’s commitment to democratizing AI and empowering every customer to unlock value from their data. Discover more in the full blog post and get ready for the future of enterprise AI.

 

The Challenge

Let’s start by facing the current reality. Business processes in enterprises heavily rely on structured tabular data. When it comes to creating predictive insights from this data, our landscape today is fragmented and siloed. While SAP has developed robust AI services tailored for structured tabular data, such as Data Attribute Recommendation for classification and regression scenarios, Personalized Recommendations delivering tailored user experiences and relevant content to support diverse business objectives and recommendation scenarios., and embedded matching scenarios (like Cash Application), each solving specific challenges effectively, these services would benefit by harmonizing into a comprehensive, unified solution for customers.

This structure has evolved naturally over time, resulting in each AI service possessing its distinct architecture and workflow. While these services are functional, SAP is actively enhancing them to streamline integration and simplify adoption for application developers. These ongoing improvements also pave the way for more rapid and seamless incorporation of cutting-edge innovations, such as the SAP Foundation Model, across our AI Services landscape.

We are excited to announce the introduction of tabular AI, a significant step forward in advancing our AI capabilities. Tabular AI will serve two key objectives: first, to unify our existing AI services tailored for structured tabular data into a single, integrated platform, and second, to expedite the integration of groundbreaking AI research, particularly in the realm of generative AI, into our products. This strategic move is aimed at not only simplifying the technological landscape but also at propelling our offerings to the forefront of AI innovation.

 

Unification

Tabular AI will unify all existing AI service offerings for structured tabular data under the umbrella of SAP’s Generative AI Hub, streamlining integration, lifecycle management, and accelerating implementation timelines. This comprehensive approach will democratize predictive AI capabilities, making them more accessible and easier to maintain. By doing so, business developers will be better equipped to effectively leverage these tools within their custom AI applications or embedded AI scenarios. As a result, business developers will be better equipped to leverage these tools in custom AI applications and embedded AI scenarios. For application developers, this unification means a simpler integration process, with a single service plan and service key required for their applications.

With this transformative change, the focus shifts from selecting a suitable AI solution for an application to concentrating directly on the desired AI functionality - be it classification, regression, matching, recommendation, or other capabilities that may emerge in the future. Notably, the inclusion of the matching capability represents a significant enhancement, as this functionality was not previously available through an existing AI service offering.

Moving forward, all these functionalities will be accessible through a unified entry point - the AI API. This consolidation simplifies the user experience and expedites the deployment of advanced AI capabilities, allowing developers to seamlessly integrate and apply these functionalities across various applications more efficiently without the challenges of managing multiple technologies or models. By eliminating barriers related to the technology stack and model selection, developers can more effectively concentrate on utilizing the precise functionality needed to add value in their specific contexts, thereby driving innovative use of AI across industries.

However, tabular AI isn't just about simplifying the developer's experience; it also paves the way for seamless integration of future innovations. A prominent example is the SAP Foundation Model, leading us into the discussion of innovation.

 

Innovation

With tabular AI in place, we are seizing the opportunity to stay at the forefront of innovation. By unifying our architecture and workflow, Tabular AI enables the seamless integration of cutting-edge technologies and native adoption of new advancements.

At launch, tabular AI will incorporate advanced AI models designed to deliver unprecedented levels of predictive insight and flexibility. Leveraging large-scale business data, these models will unlock a wealth of new use cases and transform the way users interact with our solutions.

A key feature is the SAP Foundation Model, a table-native Transformer that brings adaptive, in-context learning to tabular AI. Unlike traditional models that require repeated training for each use case, the SAP Foundation Model provides on-the-fly predictions for individual records based on provided examples. This adaptability allows the model to respond dynamically to changing data without retraining, substantially simplifying the entire machine learning lifecycle and reducing operational complexity.

Customers will benefit from increased intelligence and automation in embedded scenarios, enabling faster, more accurate decision-making. Over time, this technology will supercharge a broad range of use cases across the SAP portfolio - streamlining business processes, increasing efficiency, and reducing manual effort.

Initially, this capability will support embedded classification and regression use cases within SAP applications. This focused rollout ensures high reliability while setting the stage for expanding to broader custom scenarios in the future.

 

What's in for Me

Looking ahead to 2025, our vision is to make AI for structured data both simpler and more powerful for everyone using SAP. With the upcoming launch of tabular AI as a flagship service within SAP’s Generative AI Hub, we are unifying and streamlining all our existing capabilities by consolidating features from Data Attribute Recommendation and Personalized Recommendation and introducing matching as a native capability. Tabular AI will serve as your one-stop shop for all predictive tasks involving structured business data, providing you with a seamless and integrated user experience.

We are also taking a significant step forward by embedding cutting-edge technology: Tabular AI will feature in-context learning powered by the SAP Foundation Model. This enables highly adaptive, context-aware predictions based on real-time examples—without requiring traditional, exhaustive retraining. As your business data evolves, the model dynamically adapts, delivering relevant predictions and keeping pace with changing requirements

For the initial release of those advanced capabilities powered by the SAP Foundation Model, we are prioritizing embedded scenarios such as classification and regression tasks deeply integrated within SAP applications. By focusing on well-established use cases with proven business value, we can rigorously test and refine in-context learning, ensuring maximum reliability and robustness. This strategy will help us build a strong foundation for expanding advanced capabilities to broader and more customized scenarios in the future.

We are aiming to make tabular AI generally available in the second half of 2025.

 

Next Steps on our Journey

All of this is, however, just the beginning. Looking ahead, we anticipate a transformational leap in how advanced AI models generate insights from linked business data. Rather than requiring each tabular AI scenario to be explicitly modeled at design time, we envision a future where users can easily obtain both simple and sophisticated insights through flexible, ad-hoc predictions that accommodate real-time data changes, extensions, user interactions, and feedback. This approach will ensure the most accurate and effective insights from your business data.

These developments will also pave the way for the next generation of highly autonomous, agentic AI scenarios. Unlike today’s narrow AI models, future scenarios will support a broader range of execution and will benefit from precisely grounded insights, leveraging the foundation of structured tabular data for greater accuracy and reliability.

In effect, we expect nothing less than a democratization of AI in the structured tabular space, where a massive growth of capabilities is fueled by empowering application developers and end users alike to generate targeted AI insights as easily and swiftly as writing prompts for textual tasks performed by an LLM. As these capabilities advance, generating insights with AI will become standard practice rather than an exception - until, eventually, almost every process will be AI-powered, with only a few remaining untouched.

As part of this evolution, we will begin to phase out and deprecate our legacy AI services. This move will fully realize the unification vision: A single, universal entry point for all AI capabilities supporting structured business data. Unifying our services brings several strategic benefits:

  • Accelerated innovation: Features and updates reach you faster.
  • Streamlined integration: New advancements can be incorporated seamlessly into the existing portfolio.
  • Reduced total cost of ownership: Consolidating offerings minimizes maintenance overhead, lowering costs and increasing overall value.

For customers currently leveraging Data Attribute Recommendation or Personalized Recommendation, rest assured that there is no immediate action required. Our top priority is to ensure your business processes continue uninterrupted. We’ll reach out proactively to facilitate a smooth transition to tabular AI and to support you every step of the way.

 

Conclusion

Tabular AI marks a significant leap towards simplifying and unifying AI for structured business data at SAP. By consolidating existing AI services and introducing advanced AI capabilities like in-context learning through the SAP Foundation Model, we’re making predictive AI more powerful, accessible, and future-ready.

While the initial focus is on embedded use cases, our long-term vision is a flexible, unified offering that adapts to evolving business needs and fosters innovation at scale. This transformation is more than a technical evolution - it’s an invitation to reimagine what’s possible with your data. As we move towards a future where AI-powered insights become omnipresent, SAP remains committed to supporting your journey every step of the way.

We look forward to sharing more as tabular AI evolves. Your input will be vital as we shape this next chapter - so stay connected and be part of the journey.

Stay tuned for more updates!

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Best Practices
entry.content:
Please note that this article is part of a Dashboard Design Best-Practices blog series,
created to assist you in designing and building meaningful dashboards with SAP Analytics Cloud.
Click here to open the Dashboard Design Best-Practices blog series hub page in a new window

The focus in this blog is about Best-Practices when building  analytical applications in SAP Analytics Cloud.
If you don't have the template yet, you'll find the necessary information on how to get it in this blog post.

 -> The available links always open in a new browser tab.

 

Best-Practices

  1. Template Layout Types
  2. Layout Settings in SAC
  3. How are the SAC components nested in the Outline structure? 
  4. How users can indicate the Linked Analysis relation on a page?  
  5. Theme CSS Options
  6. Add Spacing between widgets in Logic Groups 
  7. Hide Icon on Input controls 
  8. Activate "Group Data"- Function for widgets
  9. Set "View Time" Settings 
  10. Performance Consoderations

 

1. Template Layout Types

With the new version of the SAC Fiori template, 4 pages within the template are now available.
The templates differ mainly in their layout and two different header types: Floating and Sticky Header

Floating Header (Template 1/ 2/ 4)
The floating header disappears when scrolling down, has rounded corners and a spacing of 16px to the edges or nearby layout components. It is a space-saving solution if certain information must not always be visible. 
Sticky Header  (Template 3)

The sticky header stays always visible at the top, even when the user scrolls through the content.
The header has no rounded corners and no padding to the left, right and top.
Use the subtitle section to show information that relates to the whole page. 
E.g. instructions, explanatory text, meaning of abbreviations, time periods, special conditions, etc.

The 2 headers can be used for all kind of scenarios like Start/ Home/ Sub/ Detail Pages

 

Template 1:

This template contains all the different combinations of widgets. (See also Dashboard Design Patterns Blog)
Starting with the Info Group and Control Group on Top and different characteristics of Logic Groups and single widgets with or without Input Controls. You can quickly copy and paste them or simply delete if you don't need them. 

All-in-one Template with floating header

Template 2:

Unlike Template 1, the Info Group is positioned vertically.  The info group, also called KPI row, usually provides an high-level overview on the most important numbers at a glance. Depending on the use case, this positioning may be a better alternative.

Template 3:

Template 3 differs from the others by the use of a "sticky header" and a "Collapse/ Expand Logic Group" Function.

 

Template 4:

Template 4 is intended for so-called exploration scenarios. A filter row and two chart types offer a quick way to to drill down
information using the Linked Analysis functionality in SAC and then display the details of the selection in a table.

 

2. Layout Settings in SAC

When creating a dashboard, you have a practical way to control the layout behavior of your app elements.
Use the device selection at the bottom of the SAC Canvas to simulate the possible screen size.

Make sure that the content is displayed appropriately on the end devices that the end user will be using.
The content should work visually up to a resolution of 1920x1080.

 

3. How are the SAC components nested in the Outline structure?

Element Order in Outline
While the contents of the individual sections, which are on one level, are arranged from top to bottom, the presentation in the outline is reversed. Therefore, what is at the top visually is at the bottom in the outline structure.

 

Visualized Page Structure

The next image shows the individual patterns of Template_1.
You can find more information in the Layout Patterns Blog Article.

To better understand the template structure, the structural elements as well as the relevant components of the Template_1 and their usage in the SAC outline are shown in the next picture:

  

Fiori Card Design
To achieve the Fiori card design on e.g. a panel, the CSS class name “card” is used in the styling panel of the respective panel. 

 

4. How users can indicate the Linked Analysis relation on a page?

Linked Analysis gives the user the ability to define, which other chart widgets in the analysis are affected when applying selections while browsing hierarchical data. However, to give the user a clear understanding of which widgets on the page will be affected, two scenarios are possible.

Scenario 1: 
Linked Analysis on complete page content
If a data point selection in one widget affects all other widgets on the page, it is possible to use panels for each individual widget. Since the selection affects the entire page, it is not necessary to group visually.

 

Scenario 2: 
Linked Analysis on parts of the page
If a data point selection in a widget only affects some widgets on the page, this should be visually indicated to the user. For this purpose, a (Logical Group) Panel should be used as a visual bracket of the related and influenced content

 

 

5. Theme CSS Options

When using the Fiori Morning Horizon theme, CSS classes are used from the beginning.
The Global Default Class Name "saptheme" is set automatically.
For further customization options at the component level, use the CSS class name for the corresponding component which can be found in the following table.

 

TitleWidget TypeCSS Class Name
Page TitleTextpage__title
Page SubtitleTextpage__subtitle
Page Description TitleTextpage__desc__title
Page DescriptionTextpage__desc__content
LinkTextlink
Negative TextTexttext--negative
Critical TextTexttext--critical
Positive TextTexttext--positive
Informative TextTexttext--information
Neutral TextTexttext--neutral
Fiori CardFlow Layout Panel, Panelcard
Emphasized ButtonButtonbutton--emphasized
   
   
   

 

6. Add Spacing between widgets in Logic Groups 

There is currently no option to set a padding between individual widgets.
Therefore, it can happen that widgets within a logical group are visually very close to each other.

 To avoid this, there is a workaround by adding invisible borders to the widget.

Step 1 
Select the first widget, open the styling panel in the right panel and select “Right Border”, no color value and a Line Width of 8px.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Step 2
Select the second widget in the middle, add “Left and Right Borders", no color value and a Line Width of 8px.

 

Step 3
Select the third widget, add “Left Border", no color value and a Line Width of 8px.

Result
The result is a clearer separation of the different widgets.

 

7. Hide Icon on Input controls 

Initial State
SAC first displays an additional icon when a filter or input control is created. 
For reasons of simplicity, we recommend hiding this icon. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

How-To
Open the context menu of the filter widget, navigate to the header icon ("Show/Hide -Group)
and deselect the checkmark.

 

 

 

 

Result

 

 

 

 

8. Activate "Group Data"- Function for widgets

9. Set "View Time" Settings

We recommend to use the "Present Mode" as default mode for analytical pages

1. Open Side Panel by selecting the "Story Settings" icon and the corresponding sub-items in the SAC toolbar.

 

 

 

 

 

2. Select “Present Mode” in the Navigation Panel and click the Done Button.
-> Do not forget to save the story.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

10. Performance Considerations

 

Performance of the dashboard has a big impact on the users’ experience. Poor performance results in poor user experience. When designing and building the dashboard, consider the factors that affect the performance. In general, the performance gets poorer:

  • with more data,
  • with more complexity,
  • with more data that needs to be loaded simultaneously.

Considering performance in your design will result in trade-offs. For instance, linked analysis and filters will impact the performance, yet are very useful and powerful features. You’ll need to evaluate how big the performance impact is and whether your users will be more appreciative of the power than annoyed at the wait. 

 

Reduce the amount of data used

  • If specific data is not required in the dashboard, consider
    not showing it and then don’t include the data in the model. 

 

Do aggregations early, before calculations

  • Aggregating data before it is loaded into the SAC model results in many fewer rows in the model,
    which means SAC will require less computing time to process those rows when creating the charts. 

 

Use the most performant model for your requirements

  • Typically, an acquired data model is faster than live connections.
    If you don’t need live data, consider an acquired data model. 
  • Typically, an analytical model is faster than a planning model.
    If you don’t need a planning model, consider an analytical model. 

 

Reduce the number of expensive elements that are shown together

  • Reduce the number of charts on one page. 
  • Reduce the number of high-resolution images. 
  • Use trellis charts rather than individual yet similar charts. 
  • Consider where to show geo-charts and scatterplots with many points
    as they are less performant than bar charts. 

 

Reduce the number of elements that require processing

  • Consider how many filters and what type of filters are needed, as they can impact performance. 
  • Reduce the number of widgets that are linked by the linked analysis feature,
    as that can impact the performance.

entry.title: Timer and Trigger based SAP CPI Integration
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog post, we’ll explore how to design a single CPI interface that will work with scheduled based and also on demand trigger based.

Business Scenario

In most SuccessFactors integrations, we often need to schedule interfaces based on a timer. These interfaces read messages from SuccessFactors using the last modified date timestamp and process the data to target applications. Once the data is sent to the target application, we update the last modified date and store it in a variable. During the next run, the interface reads data based on the timestamp and processes the records.

We design the interface to avoid updating the timestamp if any errors occur. For example:

  • If the target application is down or there are connection issues.
  • If mandatory data is missing for some cases.

This approach works well if the target application processes all records as a single message. However, there are situations where we need to read all employee data during a specific time interval and process each employee's data individually employee by employee. In the Splitter:

  • If we enable stop on exception it will halt all record processing.
  • If we do not enable stop on exception we might miss processing some records.

For example, if we apply a last modified date query to employment data and encounter an issue, we can correct it, and the next run will process the modified data.

In another case, if we navigate to emergency contact data from the employment portlet and apply the last modified date query on employment data, any error in the emergency contact data will prevent the record from being processed to the target. Even after updating the emergency contact, it will not process because the query's last modified date is based on employment and not on emergency contact. To handle this, we can create another integration process with a trigger-based approach in the same iFlow to query and retrieve the data, then process it from the local integration. With this trigger we can manually run the integration by passing the employee id.

Solution Architecture

 

 

SAP CPI Development 

 

In the above SAP CPI screenshot we have two integration Process

  1. Time Based Integration: This will execute based on the pre-defined time interval Steps are below explained
    • Content Modifier for Schedule Query: This one will be used to read time stamp and also scheduled query to pass in next step.
    • Local Integration Process: This one will be main local integration process, based on the query received, it will call SF and do mapping and send details to the target application.
    • Write Time stamp in the variable. This will store last modified date to process in next run.
    • Application Id, this is optional , used for additional monitoring to know from and last modified date.
    • Below is Externalized Content Modifier

 

  1. Trigger Based Integration: This will have HTTP sender and it is trigger based. An Ad-Hoc basic, team will call this end point. We can use this in some UI and give it to business to run themselves
    • Content Modifier for Trigger Query: This one will be used read parameters from trigger and form the query to next step.
    • Local Integration Process: This one will be main local integration process, based on the query received, it will call SF and do mapping and send details to the target application.
    • Additional logging.
    • Below is externalized query in Content Modifier 

 

 

Once iflow deployed it will looks like below

  • Trigger endpoint URL
  • Timer execution for schedule.

 

 

Testing

Schedule Run

In the Timer I have used Application message id to know the time interval.

Trigger Run

postman for trigger

Monitoring

 

Note in the Trigger content modifier I have used ${header.SapAuthenticatedUserName} to know who triggered iflow.

Conclusion

We can design SAP CPI ilfows to work with  both Timer based and also trigger based approaches. Depending on the requirements we can modify these as needed. Additionally,  we can provide a trigger endpoint to API or UI application for business team use.

SAP Integration Suite API Management SAP SuccessFactors Platform

entry.title: How to reset TMSADM user password
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have different TMSADM user password for ECC landscape and Solman landscape. We want to reset the password in ECC systems only.

We cannot use TMS_UPDATE_PWD_OF_TMSADM report because solman systems are part of ECC domain. So if we use this report then it will update the password of SOLMAN system TMSADM user.

So is there anyway to update the password of TMSADM user in ECC systems without touching SOLMAN system TMSADM user

entry.content:
